## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On September 3, 1939, an incredibly massive, hugely consequential, and deeply controversial geopolitical maneuver was aggressively executed by the British colonial administration. Without holding any massive, formal consultations with Indian political leaders or the heavily influential Indian National Congress, Lord Linlithgow, the Viceroy of India, officially and aggressively declared that India was at war with Nazi Germany. This incredibly massive, imperial overreach absolutely enraged Indian nationalists, fundamentally triggering the massive resignation of Congress ministries and deeply accelerating the independence movement.

On September 3, 1916, the massive, deeply influential, and incredibly vital political organization, the All India Home Rule League, was officially and aggressively founded by Annie Besant. Operating in fierce, massive tandem with Bal Gangadhar Tilak's separate league, this incredibly massive political machine heavily utilized brilliant organizational tactics to aggressively demand absolute self-government for the Indian subcontinent. The massive, highly coordinated grassroots mobilization fundamentally and heavily prepared the Indian masses for the massive, impending arrival of Mahatma Gandhi's mass movements.
